![Thumbnail (1920x1080)](https://i.ytimg.com/vi/rMB2mDm1u34/maxresdefault.jpg)
# [BMW 3 Series Head Unit Upgrade 2013-2017 | DIY Installation Guide - Q series](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rMB2mDm1u34)

**Visibility**: Public
**Uploaded by**: [Vhedia](https://www.youtube.com/@VhediaVehicleMedia)
**Uploaded at**: 2024-06-04
**Published at**: 
**Length**: 23:25
**Views**: 14170
**Likes**: 55
**Category**: Autos & Vehicles

## Description

```
Learn how to upgrade your BMW 3/4 Series (F30/F31/F34/F35, 2013-2017) head unit with CarPlay and Android Auto! This step-by-step guide covers the entire installation process, from removing the old unit to setting up the new features. Maintain your factory settings, including steering wheel controls, reverse camera, and sound system, while enjoying modern connectivity options. Perfect for DIY enthusiasts looking to enhance their BMW experience. Watch now for a comprehensive tutorial on transforming your BMW’s dashboard!

Product: https://vhedia.com.au/product/bmw-3-4-series-head-unit-f30-f31-f34-f35-2013-2017/
___________________________________________________________________________ 
SUBSCRIBE AND FOLLOW 
FB: https://www.facebook.com/Vhediavehiclemedia
IG: https://www.instagram.com/vhedia_vehicle_media/
Website: https://vhedia.com.au/
```

iTrader: ( **[0](https://f30.bimmerpost.com/forums/itrader.php?s=db709ee14139c63f351d5d8840d28f60&u=433186)**) | Almost anything is POSSIBLE, depending on how deep your pockets are... However not without a some issues...Does your car have connected drive might need a combox too. Some issues with coding (small) but still issues. If your car didn't come with NAV, you will need a separate GPS antenna and also a wifi one, depending on the year. Head unit has to be coded to your VIN...(can't use small programs like "Bimmercode" to do it either....I could be wrong on that...need to do a search.) But it CAN be done with computer programs. Might need a new harness, cable for USB in glovebox ect. If your car is HK or his was, either case speakers and amp might be also needed. Not really sure its worth the trouble, but it might be to you. You could always go apple carplay/androidauto box, a larger 8.8 factory screen and switch to the larger idrive button for cheaper and less hassle. The screen and the larger idrive button from a junkyard/crashed 3 series, but depending on your car, certain part numbers you can use, not others (screen can be coded with "Bimmer code" button is a straight swap depending on the year of your car... but carplay/androidauto box also have their little issues, but if you are not OCD, they shouldn't bother you too much. Unless of course you are getting a killer deal on the NBT EVO unit... | | | Appreciate

Hi, when you replaced your HU did you lose the features from the original unit ie;vehicle service/settings etc? | If it's another OEM unit, it will work exactly the same. If you are removing the main radio unit, then you probably would lose your settings (don't quote me though). But you can back them up to a USB stick from the profiles menu and restore them again. If it's an Android screen replacement, then you flip to the OEM, and it works like OEM, and then back to the Android side for the other apps like Android Auto and Carplay (Carplay is available on newer NBT versions). | | | Appreciate

I have a 2014 328i with the basic screen (6.5in). I found someone who totaled their 2014 BMW 328i. They are selling the screen with navigation, headunit and control knob. Would I be able to upgrade my car and is it worth it? Or am I better of upgrading just the screen and getting a software with AppleCar play?
Thank you | I have a 2014 F32 which had the Business Nav. I literally bought something off AliExpress and it's fucking brilliant. So it's the 8,8" screen (they also have the new 10" one) and it's basically a new unit, BUT you don't have to remove the old one. It's like having two infotainment systems and you switch between them by long pressing the menu button (or whatever shortcuts you may find). On the Aliexpress one i have full screen apple carplay and a lot of other shit I don't use and on the stock one i have the exact original things the car had before. Pros: system moves quick, full screen wired AND wireless apple carplay, nice and bright, really good touch screen although i only use idrive controller Cons: microphone is terrible as it is built in and you will have to explain this to all of your friends who are complaining about not hearing you properly; it does lower the audio quality by a bit but it's almost unpercievable after getting the built in equalizer settings right | | | Appreciate

iTrader: ( **[0](https://f30.bimmerpost.com/forums/itrader.php?s=db709ee14139c63f351d5d8840d28f60&u=38160)**) | Quote: | | | --- | | Originally Posted by **\_iggnat\_** [![View Post](https://f30.bimmerpost.com/forums/multisite-style/f30/buttons/viewpost.gif)](https://f30.bimmerpost.com/forums/showthread.php?s=db709ee14139c63f351d5d8840d28f60&p=28585103#post28585103)
Thank you! I never thought of that, I will try 😂 These are my first few posts on this platform although I have been using it for years. Such helpful people! | That gives you the best operation. Then the car is used for all bluetooth, and calls show up on the HUD if you have one. Catch22 is you can no longer use wireless AA/wireless carplay after doing this since it needs to run through the android bluetooth module to work. The workaround (which works a million times better anyways) is buy a AAWireless or Motorola MA-1 wireless USB dongle and plug into the head unit. You then pair your phone to that, and it looks like USB AA or carplay to the head unit. That works flawlessly. ![](https://f30.bimmerpost.com/forums/images/smilies/thumbsup.gif) | | | Appreciate

I have a 2014 F32 which had the Business Nav. I literally bought something off AliExpress and it's fucking brilliant. So it's the 8,8" screen (they also have the new 10" one) and it's basically a new unit, BUT you don't have to remove the old one. It's like having two infotainment systems and you switch between them by long pressing the menu button (or whatever shortcuts you may find). On the Aliexpress one i have full screen apple carplay and a lot of other shit I don't use and on the stock one i have the exact original things the car had before.
Pros: system moves quick, full screen wired AND wireless apple carplay, nice and bright, really good touch screen although i only use idrive controller
Cons: microphone is terrible as it is built in and you will have to explain this to all of your friends who are complaining about not hearing you properly; it does lower the audio quality by a bit but it's almost unpercievable after getting the built in equalizer settings right | Hi, Would you send a link of the item you purchased please. Thanks, | | | Appreciate

![Thumbnail (1920x1080)](https://i.ytimg.com/vi/6cNJaLLtawU/maxresdefault.jpg)
# [2004-2013 BMW 3 Series Aftermarket Radio Review & Install w/ Ashtray Delete E90](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6cNJaLLtawU)

**Visibility**: Public
**Uploaded by**: [ace789nj](https://www.youtube.com/@ace789nj)
**Uploaded at**: 2024-08-12
**Published at**: 
**Length**: 08:54
**Views**: 16992
**Likes**: 125
**Category**: Howto & Style

## Description

```
In this video I do a quick review of an all in one aftermarket radio kit along with the installation. Installation of an ashtray/pocket delete kit for button relocation and installation of OEM style back up camera. Note: with this radio kit, you do not need to purchase a separate E90 wiring harness. The kit is custom made for the 3 series, the harness it comes with plugs directly into new head unit and vehicle's wiring!

Android radio kit for E90: https://amzn.to/3WP00FI
Ashtray Delete kit: https://amzn.to/4dA5DOy
OEM E90 back up camera: https://amzn.to/3WBKIDY

Topdon automotive diagnostic tools, a must for any shop or serious DIY'ers: https://www.topdon.us/?ref=nennawqd
```

## Transcript

unit for your 2004 to 2013 3 Series BMW
that's your e90s let's get started in
order to do this on this vehicle we'll
need to remove the radio we'll need to
take this air AC controls and relocate

BMW harness for it it goes right from
the head unit to the factory wiring then
everything else you got your canbus uh
connection you've got your USB

video inputs for say a front camera
you've got your GPS antenna and a couple
other pieces then the unit itself it's a
nice size Android screen it's custom

two screw holes that'll go right into

out just tilt it down and expose the

want to take care there's a little tab

going to swing down as you swing it down

out push that out of the way and then
look at your dash you've got two screws
that hold the bottom of that radio in
you unscrew that and then you pop the

that's in the dashed there there's four
screws total on the face of it and then
there'll be two bolts inside of it
you'll see here SL Slide the radio
forward and carefully remove the
connections take caution with especially
with the small connections for the
antennas right here there's a little tab
you want to pull up and that'll allow it
to kind of unlock and you can pull them
out once the once the radio is out of
the way you have access to the four
screws that are on the face of this
little frame here you pull those four
screws out and then you're going to look
on the inside and you'll see two bolts
straight downward and that's really what
supports the back of that radio in place
and so you have to take those out as
well once youve remove those you should
be able to slide this whole frame right
out just be careful not to damage any
wires the last part of the prep for this
job is you're going to want to carefully
take the face of this trim piece here
for the air conditioning controls out
there's small tabs on the tops and on
the bottom of the unit you'll pull that
off carefully it'll expose just that air
conditioning control which is what we're
going to relocate
now here's what makes this kit from
Amazon so great it's got everything
already done for you with regard to
wiring you've got a harness that you're
just going to plug into the factory
harness and then you've got all your
other cabling that's set up for you the
only thing I'm doing manually is adding

which honestly I don't think I even need

connection of course if you've got a

there's not much you can do on this car

there's a ribbon cable on the back of

your switch pops just pops right out
there's one cable that's in there you're

way to the back into the trunk space up
into the trunk lid I'm not going to show

I'll put links to everything down below
